crossref.abstract: <jats:sec><jats:title>Objective</jats:title><jats:p>We aimed to test whether region‐specific factors, including spatial expression patterns of the tau‐encoding gene <jats:italic>MAPT</jats:italic> and regional levels of amyloid positron emission tomography (PET), enhance connectivity‐based modeling of the spatial variability in tau‐PET deposition in the Alzheimer disease (AD) spectrum.</jats:p></jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>Methods</jats:title><jats:p>We included 685 participants (395 amyloid‐positive participants within AD spectrum and 290 amyloid‐negative controls) with tau‐PET and amyloid‐PET from 3 studies (Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative, <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>F‐AV‐1451‐A05, and BioFINDER‐1). Resting‐state functional magnetic resonance imaging was obtained in healthy controls (n = 1,000) from the Human Connectome Project, and <jats:italic>MAPT</jats:italic> gene expression from the Allen Human Brain Atlas. Based on a brain‐parcellation atlas superimposed onto all modalities, we obtained region of interest (ROI)‐to‐ROI functional connectivity, ROI‐level PET values, and <jats:italic>MAPT</jats:italic> gene expression. In stepwise regression analyses, we tested connectivity, <jats:italic>MAPT</jats:italic> gene expression, and amyloid‐PET as predictors of group‐averaged and individual tau‐PET ROI values in amyloid‐positive participants.</jats:p></jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>Results</jats:title><jats:p>Connectivity alone explained 21.8 to 39.2% (range across 3 studies) of the variance in tau‐PET ROI values averaged across amyloid‐positive participants. Stepwise addition of <jats:italic>MAPT</jats:italic> gene expression and amyloid‐PET increased the proportion of explained variance to 30.2 to 46.0% and 45.0 to 49.9%, respectively. Similarly, for the prediction of patient‐level tau‐PET ROI values, combining all 3 predictors significantly improved the variability explained (mean adjusted <jats:italic>R</jats:italic><jats:sup>2</jats:sup> range across studies = 0.118–0.148, 0.156–0.196, and 0.251–0.333 for connectivity alone, connectivity plus <jats:italic>MAPT</jats:italic> expression, and all 3 modalities combined, respectively).</jats:p></jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>Interpretation</jats:title><jats:p>Across 3 study samples, combining the functional connectome and molecular properties substantially enhanced the explanatory power compared to single modalities, providing a valuable tool to explain regional susceptibility to tau deposition in AD. ANN NEUROL 2024;95:274–287</jats:p></jats:sec>
title: Combined Connectomics, MAPT Gene Expression, and Amyloid Deposition to Explain Regional Tau Deposition in Alzheimer Disease
